title: How to start a podman container on the host within a podman container?
body: Using docker, I can add "-v /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock" to mount the host docker.sock so that I am able to call **docker run** inside the container to start a sibling container on the host. But this behavior depends on that docker CLI is connecting with docker daemon. How can I achieve the same thing for podman? How can I start a container on the host from inside a podman container?

comments.body: With Podman 2.0 and higher, we provide a REST API accessible via Unix socket (default location for root being /run/podman.sock) and the ability to connect to an API server by adding the `--remote` flag to a Podman command. The API can be enabled via `systemctl enable podman.sock` if your distribution ships the default systemd unit files.
So, generally speaking, as of now you can use the same approach by mounting in the Podman socket and using the `--remote` flag or the podman-remote client, which only connects to a server.

@mheon In order to connect to the server on the host from the container, I believe I also need to install podman inside the container? Does it matter if the podman version inside the container is different from the host podman version?
For versions 3.0 and lower, yes - the version on host and in the container will need to match. 3.1 and up should not require a strict match.
